---
pagetitle: "Xorq documentation"
description: "Official Xorq documentation. Learn how to build portable, multi-engine data pipelines with deferred execution, caching, lineage tracking, and deployment-ready artifacts."
toc: false
page-layout: custom
classes: homepage
---

::: {.hero-section}
::: {.hero-content}
# Xorq

Xorq is an open source engine for portable, multi-engine data pipelines, with built-in deferred execution, caching, lineage tracking, and a git-backed catalog to publish, discover, and reuse them.

<!-- vale Xorq.Terms = NO -->
[[$]{.install-prompt} pip install xorq](how_to/install_xorq.qmd){.install-cmd title="Installation guide"}
<!-- vale Xorq.Terms = YES -->
[GET STARTED →](getting_started/quickstart.qmd){.hero-btn .hero-btn-primary}
:::
:::

::: {.content-section}

## <i class="bi bi-signpost-split"></i> Find your path

Not sure where to start? Pick the card that matches what you're trying to do.

::: {.docs-grid}
::: {.doc-card}
<i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i>

[**Tutorials**](tutorials/core_tutorials/get-started-with-xorq-init.qmd)

Learn the basics by following a worked example
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<i class="bi bi-list-check"></i>

[**How-to guides**](how_to/install_xorq.qmd)

Get a specific task done with a focused recipe
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<i class="bi bi-lightbulb"></i>

[**Concepts**](concepts/understanding_xorq/what_is_xorq.qmd)

Understand why Xorq works the way it does
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<i class="bi bi-code-square"></i>

[**Reference**](reference/)

Look up the [Python API](reference/) or [CLI commands](api_reference/cli/)
:::
:::
:::
